blocks.content: The house next door spent the morning in separate rooms.

That was new. The first two days they'd stayed together — close, overlapping, the instinct of grief to keep the herd tight. But something about the Gideon Price revelation had shifted that. People needed walls between them.

Dean went to the porch with his phone and stayed there for most of the morning, making calls Julian couldn't hear. Sophie sat on the beach — far enough down that she was a small shape against the water. Wren was in her room. Cade was in the kitchen, and Julian could hear the sound of him making things, moving pots, filling the silence with activity.

She came out the back door around nine-thirty and walked south along the beach. Slow, unhurried. She walked for twenty minutes and came back and went inside. An hour later she came out again and walked north. Same pace. Same stillness.
Everyone else is using motion to manage anxiety. Cade cooks. Dean phones. Sophie sits with the water. These are people releasing pressure.
Nadia walks the way someone walks when they're thinking. Not releasing — processing. Working through something in a specific order.
He watched her come back inside. He wrote one word on the legal pad.
